title: Why the initial statement of a for loop is not seen outside of the loop?
body:
Please, explain the behaviour of this code:
```verilog
module test();
  integer i;
  integer j;
  always @(i, j) $display("[%0t] B %0d %0d", $time, i, j);
  initial begin
    for(i = 1; i < 3 ; i = i + 1) begin
      for(j = 6; j < 8; j = j + 1) begin
        $display("[%0t] A %0d %0d", $time, i, j);
        #1;
        $display("[%0t] C %0d %0d", $time, i, j);
        $display("");
      end
    end
  end
endmodule
```
When this code is executed with `verilator --binary test.v && obj_dir/Vtest` it reports:
```
[0] A 1 6
[0] B 0 0
[1] C 1 6

[1] A 1 7
[1] B 0 7
[2] C 1 7

[2] A 2 6
[2] B 2 8
[3] C 2 6

[3] A 2 7
[3] B 2 7
[4] C 2 7

[4] B 3 8
```
B messages happen after A and before C. (ignoring the last one)
A and C messages match, so, i was expecting that B messages should match too.
